description:
A web-developer on your team left a CSV report in  
    /home/user/projects/web_analytics/raw/analytics_2023-10-15.csv  
    with the following 6-column header order:

        Date,PageViews,UniqueVisitors,BounceRate,AvgTimeOnPage

    Your job is to make a compact, tab-separated file that contains ONLY the
    “UniqueVisitors” and “PageViews” columns, in that order
    (i.e. UniqueVisitors first, PageViews second).

    1. Create the directory /home/user/projects/web_analytics/processed  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Using standard *cut* (column extraction) and *paste* or any other 
       simple coreutils you prefer (no external scripts or apps), build the file

          /home/user/projects/web_analytics/processed/traffic_summary.tsv

       The file must contain exactly five data lines – one per day – with
       a tab character between the two numbers and **no leading or trailing
       whitespace**.  There should be **no header row**.  The automated
       checker will compare byte-for-byte, so the file must look like:

            675	1450
            598	1320
            840	1703
            560	1250
            910	1800

       (Each line terminated by a single newline, last line may have a newline.)

    3. For verification purposes, also create the directory  
       /home/user/projects/web_analytics/logs and inside it a text log file
       named processing.log that contains **exactly three lines**:

            Extraction complete
            Reordering complete
            File saved to /home/user/projects/web_analytics/processed/traffic_summary.tsv

       The spelling, punctuation and line order must match exactly, and each
       line must end with a newline (including the last one).

    That’s it – two or three simple shell commands (mkdir, cut, paste, etc.)
    should be all that’s required.  Once finished, confirm the two new files
    exist and have the precise contents specified above.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the task starts:
    Path: /home/user/projects/web_analytics/raw/analytics_2023-10-15.csv
    Contents (including header row, each line ends with '\n'):

        Date,PageViews,UniqueVisitors,BounceRate,AvgTimeOnPage
        2023-10-10,1450,675,0.42,00:03:21
        2023-10-11,1320,598,0.38,00:03:10
        2023-10-12,1703,840,0.41,00:03:45
        2023-10-13,1250,560,0.40,00:03:05
        2023-10-14,1800,910,0.39,00:03:50

    Expected file after agent completes task:
    Path: /home/user/projects/web_analytics/processed/traffic_summary.tsv
    Contents:

        675\t1450\n
        598\t1320\n
        840\t1703\n
        560\t1250\n
        910\t1800\n

    Expected log file after agent completes task:
    Path: /home/user/projects/web_analytics/logs/processing.log
    Contents:

        Extraction complete\n
        Reordering complete\n
        File saved to /home/user/projects/web_analytics/processed/traffic_summary.tsv\n
